RevisionEvent.Note: Only known prior ReleaseDate of the metadata
Added HelioData InformationURL, Updated to SPASE Version 2.7.1. JWL

ResourceHeader.Description:
Middle Tennessee State University is a participant in the Radio JOVE project providing observations of radio emissions from Jupiter, the Sun, and the Galactic Background to other members of the Radio JOVE community. The observatory consists of a TFD (Terminated Folded Dipole) array and a JOVE dipole array.
The JOVE receiver is fed the output from the JOVE dipole array, the FSX 6S 12-bit switching dual polarization spectrograph receives the TFD array.

InformationURL.Name: Radio JOVE Spectrograph Users Group (SUG)
InformationURL.Description: This site provide station diagrams and other reports for the receiver systems at several radio observatories associated with the Radio JOVE project

InformationURL.Name: HelioData
InformationURL.Description: The HelioData mission page provides an overview of the mission and more, such as all SPASE dataset records associated with it.
